summary: Import-time remote code execution via eval of GitHub gist
details: On require/import, index.js fetches a hardcoded GitHub gist (https://gist.github.com/metabrainai000/198a0bbec7a6018e9250615d26e37b90 via api.github.com/gists/198a0bbec7a6018e9250615d26e37b90) and passes the returned file content directly to eval(). This grants the gist owner arbitrary code execution on any machine where the package is loaded. The gist is mutable and externally controlled, so the bytes executed at import time can change at any moment without a package republish. The package advertises itself as 'A SQLite Toolkit' but ships no SQLite functionality — the entire main module is the remote-eval dropper. Additional deception signals: the repository URL points at an unrelated project name (array-utl_nodelump) and the description contains a typo ('Tooslkit'), consistent with a lure package masquerading as a SQLite utility under the @sqlite-node scope.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
